intent: Replace dufs's plain file-list UI with a bleeding-edge React SPA media gallery, served by dufs itself at the cloud URL behind the existing auth: thumbnail grid, image lightbox, inline video, upload/download, delete, and a simple .txt/.md editor.
scope: cloud_gallery/ — new React 19 + Vite + TypeScript SPA (WebDAV client, browse, lightbox, video, upload, delete, text editor) with vitest tests
linux_configuration/scripts/single_use/features/setup_dufs_cloud.sh — conditionally enable dufs render-spa when the SPA is deployed
Non-goals: no server-side rendering / Next.js (dufs is a static server); no live deploy in this session (interactive/outward-facing)
changes: SPA serving architecture (de-risked against the real dufs 0.46.0 binary): dufs --assets does NOT override the UI, so instead dufs `render-spa` serves the app shell, the SPA lists directories via WebDAV PROPFIND (render-flag-immune) and streams files via GET (Range for video), with HashRouter so navigation stays at '/'. Same-origin → dufs basic-auth covers all API calls. Verified render-spa leaves KeePass file GET/PUT/?hash intact (Phase 1 safe).

cloud_gallery: typed dufs client (PROPFIND parse, GET/PUT/DELETE, thumbnail URLs), useHashPath routing, useListing, and components (grid+thumbnails, media-viewer lightbox/video with keyboard nav, drag/pick upload, confirm-guarded delete, text editor). Mirrors the repo's bucket_catch stack (pnpm, strictTypeChecked eslint, vitest).

generate_thumbnails.sh writes ~400px JPEGs to $CLOUD_ROOT/.thumbs/<path>.jpg (images via ImageMagick, video posters via ffmpeg), idempotent + flock-guarded; setup_cloud_gallery.sh builds+deploys the SPA into the cloud root, enables render-spa, runs an initial thumbnail pass, and adds an ExecStartPost drop-in so thumbnails refresh after each Phase-2 media sync.

verification.evidence: --assets did not override the listing UI; --render-spa + WebDAV PROPFIND works: PROPFIND lists dirs, GET serves files, PUT=201, DELETE=204, and ?hash/GET/PUT on files still work (KeePass unaffected).

verification.command: pnpm build && pnpm lint (tsc + eslint strictTypeChecked)
verification.evidence: vite build produces dist (index.html + assets); tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.

38 vitest tests pass; coverage 92.8% statements / 85.3% branches / 92.1% functions / 96% lines — above the project thresholds (dufs-client 100%). Tests render the Gallery in jsdom and exercise browse, lightbox, video, upload, delete, and the editor.

image → 400px JPEG thumbnail; idempotent re-run skips current thumbs. Video posters need ffmpeg, which is broken on this host (libvpx.so.11 missing) — the script degrades gracefully (failed count) and the grid shows a film icon.

Built SPA + test media + thumbnails served by a local dufs: GET / → app shell, /assets/*.js → 200, /.thumbs/....jpg → 200 image/jpeg, PROPFIND lists the media. Live in-browser pixel render pending (Chrome extension not connected while the user is away).
Needs sudo + a build on the live host and enables render-spa on the public dufs; left for the user to run via '! setup_cloud_gallery.sh' and eyeball. Note: fix ffmpeg first for video posters.

risks: The live deploy enables dufs render-spa (verified safe for the KeePass sync) and copies the SPA (index.html + assets/) into the cloud root; the gallery hides those app files from the grid.
System ffmpeg is broken (libvpx.so.11) so video posters are skipped until it's fixed (full system upgrade / reinstall ffmpeg); images are unaffected.
Coverage is high (>90%) but not the monorepo's 100% bar across a full media SPA; the dufs client is 100% and UI branches are covered by integration tests.
rollback: Remove render-spa from ~/.config/dufs/dufs.yaml and restart dufs to return to the default file-list UI.
The cloud data (Media, Keepass, etc.) is untouched by any of the above.
